denial_pattern_sources: incorrect_demographic_guideline: Commonwealth Fund 2023 Review of Prior Auth Denials
wrong_benefit_category: CMS Essential Health Benefits Bulletin 2022; documented in Aetna member complaints
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 02/10/2026 for Implantable loop recorder related to Recurrent vasovagal syncope (R55). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Implantable loop recorder is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Recurrent vasovagal syncope (R55). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Implantable loop recorder recommended as the next appropriate step. Three syncope events in 12 months; orthostatics and echo unrevealing. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
